def: adj. عم was common. pl. avām . 1. ( āmmā ) Common; general ( sab , 4); generic. 2. Ordinary ( sādhāran ). ām is se ki , G. G. Whether; including the case in which. ām-fahm , adj. Intelligible to all; popular. ām-kī , adj. Public; pertaining to the nation. ām log, avām, avām-ul-nās , The common people; the mass. ām māl . Common property. ām meṅ , adv. In public. ikhtiyār-i-ām , G. G. Ordinary jurisdiction; general powers. jalsa-i-ām . A general assembly; a public meeting.

n. m. 1. Doer; agent. 2. An authority; an executive or ministerial officer. 3. Revenue collector. 4. Conjuror (اوجها). Āmil māmūl kī nisbat zorāvar honā chāhiye . The operator should be stronger than his victim. āmil-i-pulis . G. G. A police officer.

adj. عود returning. 1. Alighting; resting. 2. Liable; subject to; amenable; open to; incident; obnoxious to; exposed to. āyad huā . Subjected to; laid on; charged. āyad h. , v. n. 1. To alight or rest upon. 2. To be amenable, liable, or subjected to. hiqārat āyad huī . G. G. Exposed to contempt.
more: part. act. (of عود Heb. To return) Turning towards, happening, referring to, coming back, reverting, being restored (as, money lent, &c.), returning.

def: adj. S. चाण्डाल 1. Low-born; low-cast; hybrid; mongrel. 2. Miserly ( chunchaṭa ). Woh baṛā chanḍāl hai, kisī ko bhī kauṛī nahīṅ detā . 3. A malignant; flint-hearted, blood-thirsty or diabolical creature. Nagar chhoṛ jaṅgal base, chhūṭat nahīṅ jaṅjāl, Mankhan chhāṛe kā bhayo man hī hai chanḍāl .
more: n. m. See chān'ḍāl. chanḍāl chaukṛī , n. m. A community of four or more bad men.

def: n. f. عبد worshipped. Divine worship ( bandagī ). ibādat k. , v. a. To worship (God). ibādat-gāh , n. f. A place of worship; a temple. mazhabī ibādat . Religious worship.
n. f. S. डमर मध्य ( Geog .) An isthmus.
Close; fast; intimate; thick (as friends).
n. f. pl. of خلق q. v. All created beings.
more: s. f. plur. (of خلق) Men, people, creatures, the creation.

def: adj. Intermixed; jumbled; confused; chaotic. /?/khalt malt , n. m. Jumble; disorder; confusion.

n. f. A census. /?/khānah-muaiyan . The appointed or appropriate column. khāne meṅ paṛnā , v. n. To come under a particular column. khānah-nashīn, goshah-nashīṅ , 1. One who has no employment. 2. A retired officer; a pensioner. sāhib-i-khānah , n. m. The owner or master of the house; a householder; a host.
adj. A smiling countenance.
n. m. Household goods (اسباب 17).
n. m. khān , chief sāmān things. A house-steward; butler; major-domo.

n. m. 1. Devastation; desolation; ruin. 2. Waste or unproductive land. Ab kharāba huā Jahāṅābād, Varnah har ek qadam pe yāṅ ghar thā . Mīr. The raid of Mahraṭṭās. Jahānābād is now in ruin laid, Where erst each foot of ground a house displayed.
more: 1. s. m. Devastation, ruin. 2. adj. Depraved, bad, ruined, waste.

def: n. m. 1. Costs; expenses; charges. 2. Costs of a law suit. Kharcha-i-tarfain ( yā jānibain ) zimme tarfain . ( yā jānibain ). Each party to pay his own costs. kharcha dilānā , v. a. To adjudge or award costs. kharcha-i-adālat, yā muqaddamā , Costs of suit, or of court. kharchah qābil-i-vasūl, yā yāftanī , Costs recoverable. bilā vasūl kharchah . Free of costs. dāvā maë kharcha-i-ḍigrī . Claim decreed with costs.
n. m. The small shell called kauṛī , used as coin; Cyprœa moneta. Ek kharmohrā bāqī nahīṅ rahā . Not a farthing left.
adj. Lavish; prodigal; extravagant; spendthrift.
n. m. Snoring. kharrāṭe lenā, yā bharnā , v. n. To snore.
n. m. ختن rendering imperfect. Circumcision ( musalmānī ).
wety: From Classical Persian ختنه (xatna), from Arabic خَتْنَة (ḵatna).

def: n. f. 1. The back seat of a haudah in a procession. 2. The office or functions of an attendant. khavāsī meṅ baiṭhnā , v. n. To sit on an elephant behind a great man.
more: s. m. The place where one sits behind a great man upon an elephant.

def: adj. Intelligent; sagacious; wise. khirad-mandī, aql-mandī , n. f. Wisdom; sagacity.
n. m. S. कलञ्जन Galangale; Alpinia Galanga .
more: s. m. Galangal. (Alpinia galanga or Galanga major. Sans. कुलंजन ).

def: n. f. H. chāh , q. v. Is līye bhārī janāzah hai merā, Khuāhisheṅ dil meṅ līye jātā hūṅ maiṅ . The corpse you bear is really such as weight, Because it bears its longings yet unsate. khuāhish-mand, khuāhāṅ , adj. Desirous ( ārzūmand ). khuāhish h., yā rakhnā , v. n. To be in demand or request ( chāhnā , 1, 4, 6).

r: /?/khuāh-makhuāh, khuāh na khuāh, khuāhī na khuāhī
def: adv. Perforce; nolens volens ; willy nilly; whether he would or not; ex necessitate rei ; of necessity; necessarily; willing or unwilling; on compulsion; inevitably; absolutely. 1. Aur jis kisī ke dil ko hai laṛkoṅ ke tan kī chāh, Niklā woh sāth laṛke ke kīchaṛ meṅ ho tabāh, Ulfat kī apnī chāh jatāne ko khuāh-makhuāh, Lauṅdā girā jo āge to pīchhe se yeh bhī, āh! Be-ikhtiyār uske barābar phisal paṛā . Rains. Nazīr. And those who after comely boys to lust are given up all, See with them hand in hand set out, in puddle slip and fall, The catamite perchance slips just before and down he goes, The sodomite behind just falls beside, his passion shows. 2. Chhātī se lipṭe jāte haiṅ, haṅs haṅs-ke khuāh-makhuāh, Dil bā/?/g sab ke hote haiṅ farhat se vāh-vāh! Holī. Nazīr.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian خواه مخواه (xʷāh ma-xʷāh, literally “willing (or) unwilling, willy-nilly”). Compare Bengali খামাখা (khamakha).

def: n. m. 1. A small tray. 2. A small cash box.
Ped. n. m. An applicant; a suitor; candidate; bidder. khuāst-gārī , Ped. n. f. Application; solicitation ( chāhnā ); suit.
n. f. A seed of a cooling quality ( /?/khāksī ).
n. m. A tavern; ale house; public house; gin palace.

n. m. 1. One who is popularly believed to have been a male for half the year, and a female for the other half; a hermaphrodite; a eunuch. Hazraṭ yeh nar hai na mādā, khunsā hai . 2. ( Gram .) The neuter gender (H. napunsak ).

he ate and took away. 1. Fraudulent or dishonest gains; fraud. 2. Peculations; embezzlement; misappropriation; bribes. Khurdā na burdā, muft dard gurda . Prov. Nor taken nor enjoyed, in vain the pain defied. khurd-burd k. , v. a. 1. To spend; use; enjoy; consume. 2. To embezzle. Phr. To take advantage of; lay one's hands on.
adj. S. कठोर hard. 1. Old; experienced; knowing. 2. Strong; stout. 3. Crafty; wicked; vicious.
n. f. Bread; food. Khuzādyāṅ pet meṅ paṛīṅ to kūdne lage . His stomach fed he skips elated.
adj. Unemployed.
prep. To, at, until, unto, as far as. It is the opposite of अव ava, marks the limits of action; and, prefixed to certain verbs, reverses their meaning.

r: a, fatḥa, cedar, u, fun, kasra, i, fin, ẓamma, bull., ā, father, au, ou, house, ai, aisle., rule, oo, pool, o, pole., ī, caprice, e, fête., Abjad, aśuddha, an, ananta
def: the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'
more: is the first letter of the Arabic and Persian alphabets. At the beginning of Hindī words, denoted by the characters of these alphabets, ا with the vowel fatḥa, written or more commonly understood, represents the Devanāgari letter अ a; with kasr, the letter इ i; with ẓamm, the letter ऊ u; and with the sign madda ي, the letter आ ā: at the beginning of Hindī words, moreover, when ا is followed immediately by ي, the two letters together, according to the vowel written or understood with ا, stand for ई ī ए e or ऐ ai; and when ا is followed in like manner by و, these two letters together represent, according to the vowel used with ا, either ऊ ū ओ o or औ au; but, in the middle or at the end of such words, ا stands invariably for आ ā, In the representation of numbers by the Arabic letters, ا stands for one; whence it is used also as a symbol of the deity: it signifies, moreover, the first day of…

A name of Vishnu: also, when prefixed to a word, it is an inseparable particle, signifying negation or privation; so, ادهرم अधर्म्म Injustice, from دهرم धर्म्म Justice; ادهن अधन Poor, from دهن धन Wealth.

def: E. Rus. n. f. Leavings; sweepings; rubbish; refuse; litter; grass, dung, etc. of stables; stubble; offals; filth. 1. Achchhā achchhā āp, ākhor idhar! For yourself the tit bits, for me the refuse! 2. Tobā! kyā ākhor uṭhā lāyā! Mah. Wom. Good gracious! what rubbish is this you've brought!
n. m. S. अखोट्. A walnut; the fruit of the Aleurites triloba; juglans regia . Wat.

n. m. H. astar bastar, istar bistar , (bedding, etc.). astar lining, bistar what is spread, S. वस् to cover. Chattels; goods; trifles; household stuff. Lo jī, yahāṅ se ṭahlo, apnā akhtar bakhtar samhālo . Now then, Sir, pack up and be off!

intj. An imitative word for the sound of hawking and spitting. Tush! pooh! pshaw! foh! fie! Ākh-thū, khaṭṭe hote haiṅ . Prov. "Sour grapes." ākh-thū , n. m. Spit; saliva ( thūk ). Ākh-thū terī g—ṛ meṅ, ṭakā tere hāth meṅ . Prov.
s.m. Father. (Rare.)
wety: Formed by analogy after جب (jab) and تب (tab).

ety: Old P. āw, Pehl. āp, Zend ap, S. -अप्
def: s.m. Water; water or lustre (in gems); temper (of steel, &c.); edge or sharpness (of a sword, &c.); sparkle, lustre; splendour; elegance; dignity, honour, character, reputation. — āb-āb honā, To be drenched with perspiration; to be thoroughly ashamed or abashed; to blush. — īb-utarnā, To lose lustre or polish, become dull; to rade; to lose honour, be disgraced. — āb-bigaṛnā = āb utarnā. — āb-i-bārān, s.m. Rain-water. — āb-bāzī, s.f. Play or sport in water; swimming. — āb-i-basta, s.m. Ice; glass. — āb-pāshī, s.f. Sprinkling water; watering fields, &c.; irrigation. — āb-tāb (and āb o tāb), s.f. Brightness, brilliance, lustre, splendour; dignity, grandeur, majesty, glory, pomp
more: n. m. Z. اَب S. अप्. 1. Water ( pānī ). Āb āb kar mar gayā, sirhāne rahā pānī . Prov. The water by his side, he died, crying āb, āb . [Said, by women especially, to men who flaunt learned Arabic or Persian phrases in the place of homely Hindī]. The proverb is founded on a story. 2. n. f. The lustre or water (of a gem); the temper (of steel, etc.). Is talwār meṅ kyā āb hai! 3. Sparkle; lustre; splendour; sheen. Ek nār pȳā ka bhānī, tan wā ko sagro jūṅ pānī, Āb rakhe, par pānī nāṅh, pīū rakhe hirde māṅh . Riddle ( pahelī ). Ans. Ārsī , looking glass. A female pleasant to my spouse, her body all like water clear, Bright is her face—not water though—within her breast's my husband dear. 4. Honour; character; repute ( ābrū ).
s. m. Water; splendour, lustre, elegance, dignity, honour, character; lustre or water (in gems); temper (of steel, &c.); edge or sharpness (of a sword): mode, manner.
subs: (-ko), To impart lustre (to); to glorify, praise, honour. — āb-tāba, s.m. An ewer with a spout, a jug. — āb-i-jārī, s.m. Running water, stream, rivulet; tears running down the cheeks. — āb jānā = āb utarnā, q.v. — āb-i-ioë, s.m. Water of a rivulet. — āb-jo, s.f. Rivulet, steamlet. — āb-josh, s.m. Juice of cooked meat, broth, soup, gravy. — āb ćaṛhānā (-par), To cause lustre to come (upon), to give brightness (to), to make bright, burnish, polish; to temper (steel, &c.); to whet, sharpen (a sword, &c.). — āb-i-ćashm, s.m. Water of the eyes, tears. — āb-ćashī, s.f. Giving an infant water to drink for the first time (generally when about six months old) preparatory to weaning. — āb-i-haram, s.m. Unclean or forbiddea water; wine; hypocritical tears. — āb-i-ḥasrat, s.m. Water of desire; wish, louging, appetite. — āb-i-ḥayāt, s.m. Water of life; immortality; fountain of life; a fabulous fountain (said to be veiled in obscurity); inspired knowledge ( = āb-i-ḵẖiẓr); clear, cold, sweet water; drinking water used by a king. — āb-i-ḥaiwān, s.m. = āb-i-haiyāt, q.v. — āb-ḵẖāna, s.m. Place where drinking water is kept, repository of water; reservoir. — āb-ḵẖāst, s.m. Melon, cucumber. — āb-i-ḵẖiẓr, s.m. Water of life, &c.; inspired knowledge (see āb-i-haiyāt, and also ḵẖiẓr). — āb-ḵẖur and āb-ḵẖurd, s.m. Drinking; meat and drink ( = āb-dāna and dānā-pānī), victuals; place of drinking water; fortune, lot, destiny. — āb-ḵẖora, s.m. A narrow-mouthed earthen or metal vessel for holding water (syn. kūza); goblet; cup of water

To offer a libation of cups of sherbet or milk. — āb-dār, s.m. The servant who has charge of the drinking water; — adj. Polished, bright; of a good water (as gems); well-tempered (as steel, &c.); sharp (as a sword. &c.); pure, clean, white

s.m. The place where drinking water is kept. — āb-dārī, s.f. The office of āb-dār; brilliancy (of gems); lustre, polish; sharpness (of a sword, &c.); purity, clearness, whiteness. — āb-dāna (and āo o dāna), s.m. Water and grain; meat and drink ( = dānā-pīnī), food, victuals; means of subsistence, livelihood, employment; lot, fortune, destiny. — āb-dast, s.m. Purificatory washing (such as washing the hands, &c. preparatory to prayer, or before eating, or after an unclean act); ablution; washing oneself after easing nature (this is the common signification of the word in India); a skilful workman (one whose hands turn out clean and polished work, — a rare signification); — adj. Having clean hands, pure, spotless (rare)
To wash or purify (the hands, &c.); to wash oneself after easing nature
Idem. — āb-i-dandān, s.m. Sharpness and polish of the teeth; weakness, baseness (rare)

s.m. Watering of the mouth; a species of pear; a species of pomegranate; a kind of delicious sweetmeat; — adj. Weak, vile, (rare). — āb-i-dahān, s.m. Water of the mouth, saliva. — āb-i-dīda, s.m. Water of the eyes, tears
adj. & adv. Having tears in the eyes, in tears
To have tears in the eyes, to shed tears. — āb-rū, s.f. Lit. 'brightness of face'; honour, character, reputation, rank, dignity; grandeur; pride, credit, ornament, show, appearance
(-kī), To drag down the character or reputation (of), to disgrace, dishonour; to abuse, vilify; to violate the chastity (of), to debauch; to commit an indecent assault (upon)
(-kī), To give one's honour or character to the winds, to sacrifice one's reputation; to destroy the reputation or character (of)
q.v. — āb-rū pānā, To obtain rank, dignity, or renown
(-kī), To lay one's honour in the dust; to destroy the character or reputation (of)
To pay or do honour (to); to confer rank or dignity (on), to dignify, ennoble, exalt; to sacrifice or lose one's own honour or character
To preserve one's honour or reputation
To be bent on ruining one's honour, character, or reputation
(-kī), To pay or do honour (to), to treat with respect
(-kī) = āb-rū-kā lāgū honā, q.v. — āb-rū lenā (kī) = āb-rū utārnā, q.v.
To be stained in character or reputation

Idem. — āb-i-ravān, s.m. Lit. 'Running water'; a very fine kind of muslin. — āb-rez, s.m. 'Pouring or sprinkling water'; a watering-pot; a shower-bath. — āb-i-zar, s.m. Water of gold; white wine. — āb-i-zulāl, s.m. Pure, clear, cool water; an infusion. — āb-shār, s.m.f. Water-fall, cascade. — āb-shinās, s.m. One who takes soundings in a ship, lead-man, pilot. — āb-i-shor, s.m. Salt water; sea-water; the sea. — āb-shora, s.m. Water cooled with saltpetre; a sherbet made of lime-juice and sugar (generally cooled), lemonade. — āb-i-ʻashrat, s.m. Wine; semen genitale. — āb-kār, s.m. A water-carrier; sprinkling (rare); a distiller or seller of spirituous liquors; a drinker of spirituous liquors. — āb-kārī, s.f. The business of a distiller; distillery; liquor-shop, tavern; duty on the manufacture and sale of spirituous liquors, excise. — āb-kash, s.m. A drawer of water

s.f. The act of drawing or carrying water. — āb-i-kaus̤ar, s.m. The water of the river Kausar, fabled to flow in Paradise with milk and nectar; nectar. — āb-guẕār, s.m. Passage of a river, ford, ferry; an expeditious messenger, courier; an express. — āb-guẕar, s.m. Water-course, channel for water, canal. — āb-i-gosht, s.m. = āb-josh, q.v. — āb-i-gohar, s.m. Lit. 'Water of pearls'; pure, limpid water. — āb-gīr, s.m. Receptacle for water, reservoir, pond, ditch; a weaver's brush. — āb-gīna, s.m. Lit. 'Possessed of lustre or clearness'; mirror, looking-glass; drinking-glass; bottle; — wine; diamond. — āb-i-manī, s.m. Sperma genitale ( = manī). — āb-i-nuqra, s.m. Silver reduced to a liquid state; quicksilver. — āb-nai or āb-naë, s.f. The perpendicular tube of a huqqa on which the ćilam is fixed; (in Geography) a strait. — āb-noshī, s.m. Drinking water, (e.g. ćāh-i-āb-noshī). — āb o tāb = āb-tāb, q.v. — āb o dāna = āb-dāna, q.v. — āb o rang, s.m. Lit. 'Water and colour'; beauty; splendour; polish, gloss; joy, pleasure, bliss. — āb o namak, s.m. Lit. 'Water and salt'; sufficiency of water and salt (in a dish); seasoning, condiments; ingredients; savouriness, relish, flavour, taste; — maintenance, support. āb o hawā, s.f. Lit. 'Water and air' (of a place); air, climate. — āb-yār, s.m. Waterer, sprinkler
s.f. Watering, sprinkling; — a kind of thin linen cloth; a species of dove. — āb-i-yaḵẖnī, s.m. Water in which meat has been boiled down, broth; gravy ( = āb-i-gosht).

def: v. n. 1. To perspire profusely. Rare . ( pasī'ne pasī'ne ho'nā ). Ek paṭṭī meṅ ghoṛā āb āb ho gayā . Ped. 2. To blush; to be abashed, ashamed ( pā'nī pā'nī ho'nā ). Phr. To fee! disgrace; to take shame to oneself; hang down one's head. ( sir jhukā'nā ).

v. n. 1. To become dull; to lose lustre, polish, etc. to fade. Hāth na lagāo, chākū kī āb jātī hai . 2. To lose one's honour; to be disgraced, degraded, etc. ( ābrū bigaṛnā ). Ab jīne kā dharam nahīṅ hai, sun le bāt hamārị̄, Jag meṅ jiskī āb bigaṛ gaī, zindgī huī hai khuārī . Play ( Chaubolā ). Forbid it honor I should live, hear now my words, When reputation's lost, life's but a foul disgrace.
v. a. 1. To polish; burnish; make bright. 2. To temper (steel, etc.). 3. To sharpen; give an edge to ( sāndharnā ).

n. m. 1. Meat and drink; food; victuals; water and grain. 2. Lot; fate; the hand of destiny; destiny; doom; the stars. Kahāṅ ham, kahāṅ tum, huā ye jo sāt, Ye thī bāt sab āb o dāne ke hāt . Mir Hasan. Where I, where you? that we should e'er have met together here Bespeaks the hand of Providence who metes to each his sphere. 3. Employment; service; livelihood. Hamārā āb dāna lagā hai .

adj. 1. Recent; late; modern. Ab kī, ab ke sāth; jab kī, jab ke sāth . Prov. For the present, the present matter; the future, for the future. 2. Next; another; following; coming. Ab kā sāl aisā āwe, ghar se nikle, bāhar jāwe . Astrolog. pred. The coming year will see him out of the house, a wanderer on the earth. 3. Of the present time; present; current; of the present fashion; new; fresh; new-born. ab kā zamā'na . The present time or age ( āj kal ). ab na tab . Neither now nor then; neither in this world nor the next; never; no where. Ranḍī kā dȳā ab na tab . Prov. What is given to a harlot will avail you neither here, nor hereafter.

n. f. The air and water (of a place); climate. āb o havā rās ānā, āb o hav'ā muā' fiq ā'nā yā honā , v. n. To agree with one (said of a climate). āb o havā kā bigāṛ' , n. m. A state of insalubrity or unhealthiness (of a place); an unhealthy season; an epidemic.
s. f. Water and air; climate.

n. m. lit. Water and salt. 1. A sufficiency of salt and water. Sālan* kā āb o namak dekhnā . Ped. See if there is salt enough in the soup. 2. Seasoning ( masālah ); sauce; ingredients; condiments. 3. Taste; savour; relish; flavour ( savād ). 4. Support; maintenance; food and raiment.
adv. From this time; henceforth; hence; in future; henceforward. ab se dūr , Mah. Wom. intj. Far be it from us! Heaven preserve us! God forbid! avaunt! begone!

adv. H इ this, बेर time; ता that, बेर time; S. तदा Pr. तइआ. Now and then; occasionally; sometimes. ab tab kar'nā , v. n. 1. To keep putting off; to put off; delay; procrastinate; dilly dally ( āj kal k .). 2. To evade; shuffle. Dete ho, na dilāte ho, yūṅhīṅ ab tab karte ho. ab tab ho'nā , + Wom. Mah. v. n. To be dying, or expiring. Phr. To be at the point of death; to be in a dying state; to be at the last gasp. Woh ab tab ho rahā hai, koī dam meṅ ho chukegā .

ety: prob. formed by analogy after jab, tab, &c. q.v.
def: adv. Now, presently, just now, now-a-days, a little while ago, recently. — ab-bhī, adv. Even now, yet, as yet, still. — ab-tab, adv. Now and then, presently, very shortly
more: adv. H. इ this, बेर time. 1. Now; just now; just; presently; now-a-days. Abrī ke kahalyā mor itnā kar līhīṅ . Wom. Mag. Song. Grant my request this once. 2. A little while ago; lately. ab ab kar-ke , adv. Now-a-days; recently; but lately. Pahle to chhurī kaṭārī thīṅ, ab ab kar-ke dost banī haiṅ . Mah. Wom.
(contract. of ابو) s. m. A father.
subs: To put off continually, to delay; to evade, shuffle

To be in a dying state, to be at the point of death. — ab-tak and ab-talak, adv. Up to the present time, till now; as yet, hitherto; all this time. — ab-taʼīṅ, ab-torī, ab-toṛī, ab-teṅ = ab-tak, q.v. — ab-se, adv. From this time, henceforth, henceforward, in future, hence

Far be it from us! Heaven preserve us! Avaunt! (The phrase is generally used — especially by women — to deprecate the recurrence of a past misfortune). — ab-kā, adj. (f. -kī). Of the present; for the present; this, the present; the next, the following, the coming; new, fresh
adv. This time, the next time

Idem. — ab-lag, ab-loṅ, ab-le, ab-leṅ, adv. = ab-tak, q.v. — ab-na-tab, adv. Neither now nor then; neither here (on earth) nor in the world to come; nowhere; never. — ab-hī, adv. Just now, this very moment, instantly, immediately; already.
wety: Formed by analogy after جب (jab) and تب (tab).

ety: v.n. fr. عبي, or عبأ 'to prepare,' &c.
def: s.f. A woollen cloak or habit, very loose, and open in front all the way down the centre (not unlike a boat-cloak; it is usually of a black colour with stripes, and is worn by dervises or faqīrs).
more: n. f. A cloak or habit, very loose and open in front all the way down the centre, not unlike a boat-cloak.
s. plur. Garments said to be generally black and made of goatʼs hair.
s. A sort of garment, a cloak or habit, very loose, and open in front all the way down the centre, not unlike a boat cloak ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: n. m. 1. Forefathers; (our) fathers ( bāp dādā ); ancestors; progenitors. 2. Family; race; stock; generation ( gharānā ). 3. Lineage; line; extraction ( kul ).
s.m. Fathers; forefathers, ancestors; family, race; lineage, line.
Ped. n. m. H. akār nāp . The three dimensions; length, breadth, and depth or thickness.
s.m. Adverse fortune; misfortune, ill-luck.
s.m. A swallow.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ابابیل (abābīl), from Arabic أَبَابِيل (ʔabābīl).

def: n. f. A swallow.
s.m. That which has not escaped.
more: ( अ and بچنا bachnā ) adj. That which has not escaped.

def: adj. Inhabited, populated, peopled; full of buildings and inhabitants, populous; settled (as a colony or town); cultivated; stored; full; occupied; — city, town (in this sense it commonly occurs as the last member of compounds, e.g. Faiẓābād, Ilāhābād); — flourishing, prosperous; pleasant; happy. — ābād-bashī, s.f. First assessment of newly settled or cultivated land. — ābād-rahnā, To be full, stored, flourishing, prosperous, happy or comfortable. — ābād-kār, s.m. The first settler on waste land. — ābād-karnā, v.t. To make (a place) habitable; to people, settle, found (a colony, &c.); to build and plant; to cultivate; to make (the heart, dil) rejoice, to comfort, rejoice (the heart); to build up (a house or family, ghar), beget (children). — ābād honā, To be full, flourishing, &c. ( = ābād rahnā, q.v.); to be occupied (as a house); to have the prospect of issue; to be married.
more: adj. Z. āvād, S. आवास्, P. būdan to be. (In comp.) A city; village, etc. The opposite of wīrān waste. 1. Inhabited; populous; full of buildings and inhabitants. Bhare bhare bāzār, aur galī kūṅche ābād . 2. Cultivated; peopled. 3. Full; occupied. Ābād haiṅ chhapar-khaṭ, māche, palaṅg, khaṭole . Nazīr. Filled is each bedstead, and each bed both large and small, of various sorts. 4. Prosperous; flourishing; happy ( khush ). Khānā ābād, daulat zyāda . Benediction. (Said also by friends when they break with one another, and by a discharged servant). 5. In law . Applied to a village or lands from which revenue may be levied.

adj. Cultivated, inhabited, full of buildings and inhabitants, populous, pleasant, prosperous, happy, good, well; well done! آباد کرنا ābād karnā, v. a. To build, to cultivate, to make a place habitable, to people.

def: s.m. Endless time, prospective eternity (opp. to azal, q.v.); eternity. — abad-tak, adv. To all eternity.
s.m. (pl. of بعد), Distances; divarications; dimensions. — Abʻād-i-s̤alās̤a, s.m. The three dimensions (of a solid body).
adj. (comp. & super. of بعيد), More, most, or very distant or remote; further; furthest.
more: adj. compar. (of بعید from بعد Heb. and Ch. Was distant) More or most distant.

def: n. f. Eternity; time without end.

adj. = آباد ābād, q.v. adj. Inhabited, populated, peopled; full of buildings and inhabitants, populous; settled (as a colony or town); cultivated; stored; full; occupied; — city, town (in this sense it commonly occurs as the last member of compounds, e.g. Faiẓābād, Ilāhābād); — flourishing, prosperous; pleasant; happy. — ābād-bashī, s.f. First assessment of newly settled or cultivated land. — ābād-rahnā, To be full, stored, flourishing, prosperous, happy or comfortable. — ābād-kār, s.m. The first settler on waste land. — ābād-karnā, v.t. To make (a place) habitable; to people, settle, found (a colony, &c.); to build and plant; to cultivate; to make (the heart, dil) rejoice, to comfort, rejoice (the heart); to build up (a house or family, ghar), beget (children). — ābād honā, To be full, flourishing, &c. ( = ābād rahnā, q.v.); to be occupied (as a house); to have the prospect of issue; to be married.
adv. Eternally, for ever; never.
s.f. A habitation; a cultivated, populous, or pleasant place; population; cultivation; abundance; prosperity; advancement; civilization; enjoyment, pleasure.
more: s. f. A habitation, a cultivated, inhabited, populous, or pleasant place; population, cultivation, prosperity, abundance.

def: n. f. 1. A cultivated, populous place; an inhabited, or pleasant place. Bhūke ko an, piyāse ko pānī; jaṅgal jaṅgal awādānī . Prov. 2. Civilization; culture; fine arts. ( raunaq ). Musalmānī, avādānī . Prov. Where Mahomedans are there is civilization. 3. Prosperity; advancement; flourishing state; abundance. Jis kā khāȳe an pānī, us kī kije awādānī . Prov. Bless him whose food you eat. 4. Light; lustre; life; joy. Jahāṅ jāëṅ mȳāṅ* Ramzānī. Wahāṅ hoë awādānī . Play (Bhāṅḍ kā nāch) .
For all compounds having ābād for the first member, v.s.v. آباد ābād.
adj. (f. -ā), = ابدهي abadhya, q.v. s.m. A postulate.
adj. Not to be killed; sacred, inviolable.
adj. (f. -ī), Unobstructed, unrestrained, unfettered; unoppressed; true; without difficulty; not obstructing, not opposing.
s.f. Segment of the base of a triangle.
s.m. A postulate.
adj. (f. -ā), Not deserving death, not to be slain, inviolable, sacred.

s.f. Inhabited spot or place; colony; population, number of inhabitants; cultivated place; cultivation; the part of a village lands brought under cultivation; increased assessment ( = beshī); prosperity; state of comfort; happiness, joy, pleasure.
more: n. f. 1. Cultivation; a cultivated place. 2. Extended or improved cultivation. 3. In law . The inhabited portion of the village lands, opp. of ujār . 4. Population; number of people; an inhabited place; a colony. 5. In law . Enhancement of rent; increased assessment. 6. Competency; affluence; comfort. Ashraf Zamānī khūb ābādī meṅ hai . Mah. Wom. 7. Rejoicing; happiness; merriment; revelry. Hove mubārak shādī! Jam jam, nit nit hove ābādī! Wom. Shādyānā . (Marriage Song). Blest be these nuptials evermore! Prosperity attend the pair always! 8. Light; joy; pleasure; jewel. Yeh laṛkī mere ghar kī ābādī hai . Wom.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آبادی (ābādi). Equivalent to آباد (ābād) + ـی (-ī).

def: adj. Eternal, without end, everlasting, immortal.
adj. Ped. Eternal; everlasting; immortal ( amar ).

v. a. 1. To build and plant; cultivate; make a place habitable; people; found; settle in. 2. To extend cultivation, buildings, or population. 3. To comfort; please. Dil ābād karnā. ghar ābād k.—bār — —, v. a. To sleep with one's bride for the first time; to consummate a marriage. Leṭ pahlū meṅ mere, tujh se ghar ābād karūṅ; Tujh ko lipṭā-ke gale, wasl se dil shād karūṅ . Amānat. ghar ābād honā , v. n. 1. To be occupied (a house) ( basnā ). Kirāë-dār āë, to ghar abād ho . 2. To have or get a wife; to be married. Wom. ( byāh honā ). Tumhārī beṭī kā ghar ābād ho gayā?

v. n. 1. To be prosperous, full, flourishing; to possess a competency ( harā bharā ). Bahār āī hai, bhar de bāda-i-gulgūṅ se paimāna, Rahe lākhoṅ baras, sāqī, terā ābād maëkhānā! The spring has come. Come boy, fill fill a glass of ruby wine, Flourish a hundred thousand years this boozing ken of thine! 2. To be happy, comfortable, etc.; to enjoy oneself ( khush rahnā ). Achchhā, bachchā ābād rah, khush rah! Benediction.
adj. Belonging to, or descending from oneʼs ancestors; paternal.
adj. Belonging to or descending from one's ancestors, paternal.
adj. & adv. Dumb, speechless.
adv. Downwards; below, beneath; southwards.
adj. & s.m. Without strength or power, weak, feeble, infirm, impotent, frail, enervated
adj. S. अ without, बल strength. Impotent; infirm; enervated ( abar ).
adj. (fem. of the preceding), Weak, delicate, &c.: — s.f. A woman; a delicate woman; a beautiful woman.
more: 1. adj. f. Weak, powerless. 2. s. f. A woman.

def: s.f. Weakness, debility, frailty.
s. f. Weakness.
s.m. Weakness, frailty, &c.
s.m. Weakness, frailty, &c.
s.f. = S ابلتو अबलत्व abalatwa, s.m. Weakness, &c. (see abalāpan).
s.m. = S ابلتا अबलता abalatā, s.f. Weakness, &c. (see abalāpan).
s.f. A row, range; a continuous line.
more: s. f. A row, range, continuous line.

def: adj. (f. -inī), = ابل abal, q.v. adj. & s.m. Without strength or power, weak, feeble, infirm, impotent, frail, enervated
n. m. A bird resembling a mainā with a white breast.
s.m. = اولوکن avalokan, q.v. s.m. Looking at, beholding; sight, view, inspection.
s.m. The eighth month of the Persian year.
more: s. The eighth Persian month, when the sun enters Scorpio.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آبان (ābān), related to آب (āb, “water”).

def: s.m. This side, the near bank of a river.
adv. Now, at present ( = اب ab); — adj. Weak, &c. ( = ابل abal).
adj. 1. Coarse; clumsy; rough. 2. Indistinct; not musical. Bājā abaṛ dhabaṛ baj rahā hai .
E. adj. Weak; powerless; feeble. Phr. Weak as a child; weak as a baby; weak as a chicken; weak as a cat. Sher jo haigā babar. Laṛe Dayā Rām se abar . Song. Kaṛkā Dayā Rām .
adj. & adv. Clumsy; coarse, rough: out of all time and tune, jarring, discordant, inharmonious, confused (music).
s.m. = آبهرن ābharan, q.v. s.m. Jewels, ornaments; decoration, finery. (The popular form is abran.)
= اورشن avarshan, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Unsubmissive to another's will, disobedient; unrestrained; unsubdued; not under control: not having one's own free will; in subjection to another; helpless, powerless; without choice.
q. v.

ety: inf. n. of عبث 'to play, sport'
def: s.m. Play, sport, trifling, frivolity, vanity, absurdity; — adj. & adv. Trifling, frivolous; vain, idle, absurd, nugatory, profitless, bootless; — in vain, uselessly, bootlessly, idly, absurdly
more: adj. Nugatory. abas , adv. Without force; to no purpose ( birthā , 2); bootless; in vain.
1. adj. Vain, idle, absurd, trifling, profitless. 2. adv. In vain, idly, absurdly, uselessly. 3. subst. Vanity, absurdity.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Without clothes, naked.
s.m. Father; superior; — a consummate knave. — abbā-jān, abbā-jānī, dear father.
more: n. m. Z. آب آبو Old P. بابا Z. and H. bāp , H. bāvā, bāpū, bābā , A. آبو 1. Father; papa. 2. A superior; a consummate knave. ab'bā jān,—jā'nī,—jī , n. m. Dear father; dear papa. Abbā jī kā jī achchhā nahīṅ .

def: (intens. n. fr. عبس 'to look stern,' &c.), s.m. lit. 'Having a stern or morose countenance'; a proper name; — the plant Marvel of Peru, Mirabilis jalapa.
more: s. m. Name of a plant, marvel of Peru. (Mirabilis jalapa).

def: adj. Of the colour of the flower of the Marvel of Peru; red; — s.f. The flower of the Marvel of Peru ( = gul-ě-ʻabbās).
more: adj. Red (color). gul-i-abbāsī ; Pop. gulābāsī , n. f. The marvel of Peru; mirabilis jalapa .
adj. Red (colour). s. f. Name of a flower. See عباس aﻌbbās or گل عباس gul-i-aﻌbbās under گل gul.

def: s. f. Play or sport in water; swimming.
For compounds having ab for the first member v.s.v. اب ab.
more: adv. Yet; still; even now; as yet.

def: s.f. = اپسرا apsarā, q.v. s.f. A female dancer in the court of Indra; a nymph; a courtesan. — apsarā-swar, s.m. The notes or echoes of the apsarās.
s. f. The giving drink to a child for the first time (generally when about six months old) preparatory to weaning.

ety: v.n. fr. عبد 'to serve'; see ʻibādat
def: s.m. A slave, bondsman; a servant (of God), a worshipper; a devotee
more: (Heb. ) s. m. 1. A servant of God, devotee. 2. A slave.
subs: Servant of God; a proper name.

def: s.m. Cloud; year, æra.

(pl. of بدل badal, or بديل badīl), m. Lit. 'The substitutes or lieutenants'; — 1˚. Certain righteous persons of whom the world is never destitute, and by whom God rules the earth: 2˚. The substitutes and successors of the prophets: (hence,) 3˚. A religious person, hermit, devotee, saint, enthusiast: 4˚. A religious fraternity; a class of wandering Mohammadan saints.
more: s. m. (plur. of بدیل) A religious person, a devotee, an enthusiast.

def: adj. Of or relating to an abdāl or devotee.
s. plur. (of بدن) Bodies.
n. m. P. آب water دار keep. A servant in charge of the drinking water, etc.
more: adj. 1. Polished; of good water (as gems); well-tempered (as steel); sharp (as a sword). 2. White; pure; clean. Miṭhāī kyā ābdār hai!

(آب q. v. and دار Having) s. m. The person entrusted with the charge of water for drinking: any thing full of water or splendour, as fruits, jewels, pearls, swords. adj. Polished, clear, of a good water (as gems), well tempered (as steel), sharp (as a sword).

def: n. f. 1. The office of an āb-dār. Rare . 2. The brilliancy (of gems); polish; lustre. 3. Purity; whiteness; clearness. Āb-dārī kā mol hai .
more: s. f. The having water; the brilliancy (of gems), temper or polish (of steel), sharpness (of a sword).

def: n. m. Māṛ. paranḍā , W. palaiṅ'ḍī . The place where drinking-water is kept.
s. The place where the āb-dār keeps the water for drinking.

Mah. n. m. P. دست Old P. and Z. zasta , S. hasta hand. Washing the anus after evacuation. āb-dast le'nā,—kar'nā , Mah. hāth pānī lenā , Hin. v. n. To wash the anus after evacuation. (gāṅṛ dhonā). Āb-dast le-kar paejāmā bāṅdh lo .
more: s. m. Water for washing the hands; washing the hands; a masterlike work-man who imparts lustre to what he executes; a guiltless faḳīr. adj. Having pure hands.

def: s.f. Segment of the base of a triangle.
s.m. The ocean. — abdhi-kaph, s.m. Cuttle-fish bone.
s.m. One who has separated himself from worldly feeling and obligation; a Hindū faqir, a jogī (q.v.); a devotee; (commonly) a worshipper of the god Śiva.
more: s. m. ( अवधुत ) One devoted to God but not attending on ceremonies; a kind of Hindū devotee who worships Siva.

def: n. m. S. अव down, off, धू to shake. A perfect (sidh) Jogī; a Hindū faqīr who professes to have "shaken off" the infirmities of humanity; one who devotes himself to God alone, rejecting all ceremonies and worship; a Hindū devotee; a worshiper of Shiva who indulges in intoxicating drugs, as Shiva (Mahādeo) is said to have done. Bhaṅg pīë, mauj kare, banā rahe abdhūt . Song. Lāonī .
s. f. ( अवधुत ) One devoted to God but not attending on ceremonies; a kind of Hindū devotee who worships Siva.
adj. Ped. In tears; with tears in one's eyes; the tears standing in (one's) eyes, or starting from the eyes.
more: s. Tears. āb-dīda, adj. In tears, weeping.

def: v. n. To have tears, or tear drops, standing in one's eyes ( āṅkheṅ ḍabḍabānā ).
s.f. The being a slave, the state or condition of a slave, servitude, slavery.
interj. Holloa! ho there! you fellow! Sirrah! you rascal! — abe-tabe-se pesh ānā, v.n. To address (one) with undue familiarity, or disrespectfully or rudely; to treat (one) ceremoniously
more: intj. 1. Hallo; you fellow; hark ye; ho there; avast; hold hard; woh; ho! 2. Fellow; friend; sirrah; you rascal! abe tabe se pesh ānā, yā kar'nā, are tare —, v. a. 1. To thee and thou a person; to address one rudely or disrespectfully, as fellow, sirrah, etc. 2. To treat unceremoniously, without due deference or courtesy. 1. Abe tabe se wohī log pesh āte haiṅ, Jo subah shām ko uṭh-kar salām karte the . Those thee and thou me now to whom they once respect did shew, Each morn and eve stood up, salamed , and made obeisance low. 2. Abe tabe na karo, nahīṅ lām kāf kī ho jāëgī . Don't thee and thou me, or it will come to downright abuse soon. abe wābe , intj. Slang . Bravo! well done! Abe wābe maiṅ! Prov. Nobody like me.

def: s.f. Lateness, delay
subs: after time; late in the season; unseasonably.

def: (Opp. of saber early), adv. S. अ not, वेला time. Late; too late; after time; behind hand; late in the season. Phr. At the eleventh hour; a day after the fair; after meat mustard; after death the doctor; late in the day; late at night.
more: n. m. S. अभ्रक. A powder of talc mixed with gulāl (red powder) used by Hindus in the Holi festival.

def: v.s.v. ابے abe.
s. m. Glass, a mirror, a looking-glass, a drinking glass; wine; a diamond.
s. A ditch in which water stands, a pond, an instrument like a broom for sprinkling water on a warp stretched for weaving.
s. m. A water-passer, an expeditious messenger, a courier, a passage of a river, a ford.
s.f. Light, splendour, beauty.
adj. Fearless; without fear; free from fear; bold; plucky; courageous; brave; daring; dauntless; stout-hearted; unflinching. Dīn jān-ke abhaë karīje .
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अभय (abhaya). Equivalent to اَـ (a-) + بَھے (bhai).

def: s.m. Unfortunateness; misfortune, calamity, ill-luck.
more: Ped. n. m. S. अ not, भज् to share, H. bhāg portion. 1. Ill-luck; adverse fortune; mischance; mishap; misadventure; cross. 2. Reverse; adversity; misfortune; calamity.

def: (f. -ī), = ابهاگی abhāgī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Having ill-luck, unfortunate, unlucky, ill-starred; destitute: s.m. An unfortunate or unlucky wight.
more: n. An unfortunate, destitute person; a poor wretch; a scape-grace. Jab se yeh abhāgā janmā, sāre ghar meṅ jhāṛū phir gaī . Hin. Wom.

def: s.m. Unfortunateness, ill-fate, lucklessness.
adj. (f. -inī), Having ill-luck, unfortunate, unlucky, ill-starred; destitute: s.m. An unfortunate or unlucky wight.
s.m. = ابهاگ abhāg; — adj. (f. -ā), = ابهاگی abhāgī, q.v. s.m. Unfortunateness; misfortune, calamity, ill-luck.
adj. (f. -matī), = ابهاگي abhāgī, q.v. adj. (f. -inī), Having ill-luck, unfortunate, unlucky, ill-starred; destitute: s.m. An unfortunate or unlucky wight.
part. adj. Not to be eaten; unlawful to be eaten: — s.m. Unlawful or forbidden food.
adj. (f. -ā), Unbelieving, undevout; unattached, indifferent. — s.m. An unbeliever, &c.
more: s. f. Want of desire or faith, disregard, indifference.

def: s.f. Want of devotion to; want of faith; unbelief, impiety; disregard; want of desire; indifference.
adj. & s.m. (f. -matī), Undevoted to; unbelieving; indifferent to: — An unbeliever.
s.m. A cloud. — abhāl-ānā and abhāl ćhānā, v.n. To be clouded or overcast.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi ابھال (abhal) (c. 1592) / آبھال (ābhal) (c. 1638), from Sanskrit *अभ्रतल (*abhratala, “sky”) (अभ्र (abhrá) + तल (tála)) or Sanskrit *अभ्रकाल (*abhrakāla, “cloudy weather”). Cognate with Marathi आभाळ (ābhāḷ).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Unbefitting, unbecoming, unsuitable: distasteful; unacceptable.
adj. Not breakable; strong; indestructible, perishable; not broken.
s.m. Non-existence; absence; extinction, annihilation; poverty, want, destitution

n. m. S. भू to be. 1. Non-existence; annihilation; death; extinction; disappearance. 2. Absence; want; destitution; poverty. Rājā ke ghar meṅ motyoṅ kā abhāo! E. Prov. A king's palace, and a scarcity of pearls!
adj. (f. -ā), Light, of little weight.
s.m.(?) The narcissus; jasmine; anemone.
adj. (f. -ā), Without credit, respect, or character. — abharam karnā, v.t. To dishonour, disgrace.
more: adj. Without credit or respect or character.

def: s.m. Jewels, ornaments; decoration, finery. (The popular form is abran.)
s.m. Jewels, ornaments; decoration, finery. (The popular form is abran.)
adj. Of or relating to the narcissus, &c.; — s.f. = ʻabhar.
s.m. Appearance, semblance, likeness; semblance of a reason; fancy; thought; impression, idea; intention, purpose.
s.m. A little; a few.
s.m. Introduction, preface, preamble, exordium
